blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I've thought about this a thousand times. This night. What I'd say. How it would go. And I don't have anything prepared. No speech. No plan.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I know. I just — I need you to know that none of this has been accidental. Not a single bit of it. Since we were twelve years old in this same field. None of it has been accidental for me, Daisy.
She turned to look at him.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You are the most important thing. Not just in my life — you are the thing my life has been pointed at since I was old enough to understand what that meant. I should have said it clearer, sooner. I was scared. We both were. But I think we were also — careful. And I don't regret the careful. I just don't want to wait anymore.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I'm here. In this field. In the same place where all of it started. And I need to say it out loud right here, tonight — not across two thousand miles, not on a porch in February, not over the phone. Here.
She looked at him for a long moment.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I've been in love with you for a long time, Kit.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I didn't say it because saying it felt like standing at the edge of something I couldn't come back from. And I needed to be sure. I needed to know it wasn't just the closeness or the comfort or the way we fit. I needed to know it was actually you.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: It's you. It has been for a long time. It's just you.
